note: create-1k: octane must not fall to >2x React's create time (known ~0.8-1.0x; generous headroom).
partial update: octane vs React (known ~1x).
swap rows: LIS keyed reconcile vs React (known <1x).

TSX/TSRX flat-list control: create-10k stayed within 1.03-1.13x across seeded normal and reversed target orders through 2026-07-19. The 1.25 ceiling protects the shared keyed forBlock lowering without relying on sub-millisecond ops.

TSX/TSRX flat-list control: seeded quick clear-10k runs measured up to 1.14x through 2026-07-19 with high three-sample teardown variance. The 1.3 ceiling keeps useful headroom while catching a dialect-specific cliff.

rotate-backward is the LIS-vs-lastPlacedIndex differentiator: octane moves 1 node, React moves all survivors (known ~12x faster → 0.08x). Guard 0.6 leaves huge headroom while still catching a fast-path break.
Cold production mount of both 1000-row walls (2000 rows total): Octane and React are currently near parity (~0.95x); 1.5 leaves standard noise headroom while catching a structural mount regression.
Production autoMemo skips the unchanged RowsA region before the keyed wall; if dependency-scoped reuse breaks this rises toward the vanilla React memo-wall cost.
TSX/TSRX component-heavy mount control. Adaptive paired runs stayed at 1.22-1.32x through 2026-07-19; 1.6 leaves browser/JIT headroom while keeping the dialect gap bounded.

Known return-JSX autoMemo gap: the calibrated 8ms batches measured 469-491x through 2026-07-19. This intentionally loose ceiling catches added work until descriptor-output reuse closes the gap; deterministic work gates remain the primary oracle.
Known return-JSX one-change gap measured 21.2-25.9x through 2026-07-19. The 32x ceiling allows normal paired variation while preventing more row/list work from landing.
Context-through-wall TSX/TSRX ratio remained 2.24-2.37x through 2026-07-19. The ceiling protects leaf-only refresh behavior while the return-JSX wrapper work is optimized.
Order-balanced A-B-B-A TSX/TSRX mount guard. Current and historical production ratios are 1.55-1.79x; 2.25 leaves cold-page noise headroom while catching more component-boundary scaffolding.

Order-balanced A-B-B-A full context fan-out guard. Current and historical production ratios are 2.28-2.94x; deterministic work gates separately pin blocks, descriptors, keyed survivors and 1024 text writes.
Order-balanced A-B-B-A full teardown guard. Current and historical production ratios are 4.05-5.25x; the ceiling catches growth while deterministic work gates bound block/scope teardown.
Order-balanced A-B-B-A 100-component chain mount guard. Current and historical production ratios are 1.19-1.41x; 1.8 leaves cold-page noise headroom.
Order-balanced A-B-B-A shallow cascade guard. Current and historical production ratios are 3.0-4.13x; precise work gates separately require one text write and cap every extra block/descriptor path.
full per-cell update churn: octane's per-binding diff vs React (known <1x).
octane server render() vs react-dom renderToString at 50 cards (known ~0.6x → octane faster). Guard 1.2 tolerates noise but catches an octane SSR regression.
intra-octane de-opt cliff: plain-.ts createElement vs compiled .tsrx SSR (known ~2.1x). Bounds the cliff so a broken fast path (ratio→1) or a runaway deopt (ratio>4) both surface.
Deterministic TSX/TSRX total-gzip gap. Two clean production runs on 2026-07-19 were byte-identical at 33765 / 18754 = 1.8004x; 1.82 catches retained runtime graph growth while allowing improvements.
Deterministic TSX/TSRX framework-gzip gap: 30974 / 16407 = 1.8879x on 2026-07-19. This isolates the generic descriptor/runtime graph from application code.
Deterministic TSX/TSRX application-code gzip: 2791 / 2347 = 1.1892x on 2026-07-19. Keeping this separate prevents a framework-size win from hiding generated app-code growth.

Rows TOTAL gzip vs ripple, same app, normalized minify. Optional Hydrate/ViewTransition and checked-restoration capabilities reduce the current runtime to 18545 bytes. The resulting 18545 / 12814 = 1.447x record is guarded at 1.47x.
Rows TOTAL gzip vs solid, same app, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 18545 / 14732 = 1.259x on 2026-07-18; the ceiling remains 1.28x.

gzip(minified compiled corpus) vs gzip(source corpus). The committed 2026-07-15 record was stale: a clean base build measured 24526 compiled bytes, while the current compiler reduces that to 24397. The resulting 24397 / 23519 = 1.037x record is guarded at 1.04x.
Production compilation of the dynamic/spread/createElement sentinel must be byte-identical with and without native-change analysis.
Minified production sentinel output must retain zero diagnostic codegen cost.
Gzipped production sentinel output must retain zero diagnostic codegen cost.

Rows APP-CODE gzip vs ripple, normalized minify. A clean base and the current build both measure 2355 bytes, correcting the stale committed record; 2355 / 2312 = 1.019x on 2026-07-17 is guarded at 1.03x.
Rows APP-CODE gzip vs solid, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2355 / 1991 = 1.183x on 2026-07-17; the ceiling is rebaselined to 1.19x.
TodoMVC APP-CODE gzip vs solid, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2046 / 1292 = 1.584x on 2026-07-18; the ceiling remains 1.59x.
TodoMVC APP-CODE gzip vs ripple, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2046 / 1696 = 1.206x on 2026-07-18; the ceiling remains 1.21x.
chat-stream APP-CODE gzip vs solid, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2630 / 2103 = 1.251x on 2026-07-17; the ceiling is rebaselined to 1.26x.
chat-stream APP-CODE gzip vs ripple, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2630 / 2336 = 1.126x on 2026-07-17; the ceiling is rebaselined to 1.13x.
Rows TOTAL production gzip vs Svelte 5, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 18545 / 18281 = 1.014x on 2026-07-18; the ceiling remains 1.03x.
Rows APP-CODE production gzip vs Svelte 5, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2355 / 2301 = 1.023x on 2026-07-17; the ceiling is rebaselined to 1.03x.
TodoMVC TOTAL production gzip vs Svelte 5, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 19744 / 18481 = 1.068x on 2026-07-18; the ceiling remains 1.07x.
TodoMVC APP-CODE production gzip vs Svelte 5, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2046 / 1511 = 1.354x on 2026-07-18; the ceiling remains 1.36x.

chat-stream TOTAL production gzip vs Svelte 5, normalized minify. Optional checked/radio restoration keeps the unused graph out of this fixture; the current record is 19753 / 19123 = 1.033x on 2026-07-18, within the 1.04x ceiling.
chat-stream APP-CODE production gzip vs Svelte 5, normalized minify. The current deterministic record measured 2630 / 2279 = 1.154x on 2026-07-17; the ceiling is rebaselined to 1.16x.

Weather app TOTAL production gzip vs React 19, normalized minify. Component flags plus optional Hydrate/ViewTransition and checked-restoration capabilities reduced the deterministic record from 50737 to 40981 bytes after merging current main; 40981 / 65725 = 0.624x on 2026-07-18.

Weather app APP-CODE production gzip vs React 19, including each port's shared weather service and formatting modules. The current record measured 7802 / 5291 = 1.475x on 2026-07-18; the 1.6x ceiling bounds compiled app growth separately from framework runtime.

Weather app FRAMEWORK production gzip vs React 19, normalized minify. Component flags plus optional Hydrate/ViewTransition and checked-restoration capabilities measured 33179 / 60434 = 0.549x on 2026-07-18; the tight deterministic ceiling catches a feature graph being retained again.

Paired desktop Lighthouse FCP under simulated Dense 4G. The six-target five-sample record measured 362.47 / 362.37 = 1.000x on 2026-07-17; the broad ceiling catches structural load regressions without treating shared-runner noise as signal.

Paired desktop Lighthouse LCP under simulated Dense 4G. The six-target five-sample record measured 403.71 / 403.55 = 1.000x on 2026-07-17; the broad ceiling catches structural load regressions without treating shared-runner noise as signal.

Paired desktop Lighthouse Speed Index under simulated Dense 4G. The six-target five-sample record measured 362.47 / 362.37 = 1.000x on 2026-07-17; TBT and CLS are intentionally unguarded because either reference can legitimately be zero.

10-level nested-use() init vs React 19. Compiler-parallelized use (docs/suspense-parallel-use-plan.md, recorded 2026-07-09) collapses the waterfall to the parallel floor: measured 0.065x (20.1ms vs 307.3ms). Guard at 0.25x so a regression back toward per-level rounds fails loudly.
transition-driven refetch vs React 19 (measured 0.10x on record 2026-07-09 - 19.1ms vs 190.1ms, parallel refetch via warm adoption).
init vs Solid 2.0 (parallel by model): measured 1.06x on record 2026-07-09 (20.1ms vs 19.0ms). The compiler's waterfall-elimination win must not regress.
init vs ripple (parallel by model): measured 1.05x on record 2026-07-09 (20.1ms vs 19.2ms).
update vs Solid 2.0: measured 1.03x on record 2026-07-09.
update vs ripple: measured 1.09x on record 2026-07-09.

Cross-framework cold-mount topology guard: Octane reaches the honest 2-wave dependency floor vs React's 6 waves in the 2026-07-17 record. The 1.5x ratio headroom allows reference improvements; the suite separately enforces Octane's exact seven-resource independent first wave and eight-call ceiling.

Cross-framework transition topology guard: Octane reaches the honest 2-wave dependency floor vs React's 3 waves in the 2026-07-17 record. The 1.5x ratio headroom allows reference improvements; the suite separately enforces Octane's exact seven-resource independent first wave and eight-call ceiling.
Deterministic mounted-tree scaffolding ceiling: TSX 11263 / TSRX 3081 nodes on 2026-07-19. Visible element/text controls below prevent dropped output from looking like an improvement.
Return-JSX boundary-marker tripwire: TSX 8192 / TSRX 10 comments at mount. This is a max-only gap ceiling so direct-return lowering can reduce it without rebaselining.
Semantic control for the recursive mounted census: both dialects render exactly 2047 visible elements.
Semantic control for the recursive mounted census: both dialects render exactly 1024 text nodes.
Deterministic partial-teardown scaffolding ceiling: TSX 10920 / TSRX 2984 nodes after removing the 32-leaf subtree.
Partial-teardown marker tripwire: TSX 7944 / TSRX 8 comments. Max-only so intended marker removal passes.
Semantic control after partial teardown: both dialects retain exactly 1984 visible elements.
Semantic control after partial teardown: both dialects retain exactly 992 text nodes.
Deterministic 100-component-chain scaffolding ceiling: TSX 418 / TSRX 319 mounted nodes on 2026-07-19.
Return-JSX chain marker tripwire: TSX 208 / TSRX 109 comments. Max-only so direct component lowering can reduce it.
Semantic control for the signal-chain census: both dialects render exactly 100 visible elements.
Semantic control for the signal-chain census: both dialects render exactly 110 text nodes.
Deterministic DOM census at 1k rows. Marker reuse leaves Octane at 10074 vs React 10072 nodes (2026-07-13); catches structural-node regressions without timing noise.
Semantic control for the node census: equivalent fixtures must render exactly the same 8051 elements.
Semantic control for the node census: equivalent fixtures must render exactly the same 2021 text nodes.
A stamped sole-component row must share its host boundary: TSRX naive and tuned now both render 10074 nodes instead of the naive fixture carrying 2000 extra comments.
Return-JSX single-root stamping plus keyed boundary sharing removes the JSX naive fixture's 4000 extra comments; its DOM shape must stay equal to tuned.
100-item TodoMVC DOM census: Octane 730 vs React 623 nodes after dormant-if/list-anchor elision (1.172x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: equivalent TodoMVC states render exactly 517 elements.
Semantic control: equivalent TodoMVC states render exactly 106 text nodes.
Cold-context navigation through the mount effect and mock fetch. The 1.5x ceiling leaves broad browser/network noise headroom while catching a material Octane boot regression.

Thirty-six native click-driven keyed forecast updates. The pre-guard quick run recorded 3.50 / 5.60 = 0.63x on 2026-07-17; the 1.5x ceiling tolerates sub-millisecond commit noise while catching a reconciler or hook-update regression.

Collapsed weather-app DOM census: the pre-guard run recorded Octane 237 vs React 184 nodes (1.288x) on 2026-07-17. The 1.4x ceiling bounds compiler-anchor overhead beside the exact observable snapshot gate.
Mixed chat conversation census: Octane 104 vs React 80 nodes after host-vs-host keyed-item boundary sharing (1.30x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: equivalent chat states render exactly 55 elements.
Semantic control: equivalent chat states render exactly 25 text nodes.
Closed portal swarm #main census: Octane 2622 vs React 2014 nodes after component-item and dormant-if elision (1.302x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: closed portal fixtures render exactly 1411 elements under #main.
Semantic control: closed portal fixtures render exactly 603 text nodes under #main.
Open portal swarm whole-body census retains load-bearing foreign target pairs: Octane 7025 vs React 5217 nodes (1.347x, 2026-07-13).
Semantic control: open portal fixtures render exactly 3412 elements across main and foreign targets.
Semantic control: open portal fixtures render exactly 1805 text nodes across main and foreign targets.
Production-browser mount of 1,000 real Three meshes. The refreshed 10-sample record is 2.64 / 2.52 = 1.05x; a 100-sample stability run recorded 2.612 / 2.655 = 0.98x on 2026-07-17.

In-place position update of 1,000 retained meshes. The refreshed 10-sample record is 0.68 / 0.58 = 1.17x; a 100-sample stability run recorded 0.525 / 0.510 = 1.03x on 2026-07-17, within the sub-millisecond timer noise floor.
Keyed reverse with survivor identity retained. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 3.30 / 2.02 = 1.63x on 2026-07-17.
Removal of the rendered 1,000-mesh tree, excluding delayed root-registry teardown. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 1.84 / 2.12 = 0.87x on 2026-07-17.
Constructor reconstruction plus observed disposal for 1,000 objects. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 9.28 / 2.00 = 4.64x on 2026-07-17.
Per-frame cost for 1,000 useFrame subscribers, averaged over 20 manual frames. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 0.059 / 0.032 = 1.84x on 2026-07-17.
Native pointer dispatch and deduplicated Three raycast delivery to 40 overlapping targets. The refreshed observable-commit baseline recorded 0.348 / 0.327 = 1.06x on 2026-07-17.
Minimal direct-root production gzip, with built bundles executed and scene-checked in Chromium. The refreshed observable-scene baseline recorded 305165 / 188008 = 1.62x on 2026-07-17.
Full-catalogue production gzip, with namespace retention and a real Mesh checked in Chromium. The refreshed observable-scene baseline recorded 304757 / 250306 = 1.22x on 2026-07-17.

Per-island delegated-listener cost must stay flat as island count scales (currently exactly 1.0x at 5 listeners/island). Growth here means a super-linear structural regression in delegation-target registration; Phase 5's selective hosted delegation may only LOWER the empty-island cost.
Exactly one owner-bridge binding per hosted root regardless of island count (currently 1.0x). Growth means duplicated bindings or a leaked rebind per render.

Deterministic source-level allocation gate: a 12-cell visible window over 1,000 logical rows currently allocates 0.012x as many physical cell roots as an eager one-cell-per-row model. The 0.02 ceiling catches loss of bounded native recycling without relying on timing.

Fresh-process parse+eval of the built server entry: octane's bundled runtime.server imports ~6x FASTER than react-dom/server (0.17x on 2026-07-20). 0.6 protects that cold-start advantage while leaving room for runtime growth.
Raw-renderer cold TTFB behind identical node:http hosts: octane measured 0.89x react on 2026-07-20 (41.6ms vs 47.0ms). A breach means a renderer-side cold-start regression, not host noise.

Warm shell first-byte over HTTP on the streaming page: parity with Fizz (1.02x at ~0.37ms, rme ~15%) on 2026-07-20. Guards the render-whole-shell-before-first-write design from regressing further; sub-ms op, hence generous headroom.

Warm sequential HTTP request total on the all-fast page (throughput inverse): 1.10x react on 2026-07-20 with high sub-2ms variance. Catches a per-request streaming-engine cliff (per-wave re-render bookkeeping) without pinning noisy sub-ms deltas.
Start-app cold TTFB behind line-for-line identical minimal hosts: octane measured 0.65x react on 2026-07-20 (60ms vs 93ms spawn-to-first-byte). Guards the app-layer cold-start advantage.

KNOWN GAP under active investigation: warmed /posts first-byte measured 2.57x react on 2026-07-20 (1.85ms vs 0.72ms) — the octane Start SSR stack, not the host (nitro-vs-minimal ~1x) and not the raw renderer (ssr-http shell is at parity). The 3.5 ceiling only catches further regression; renderer/Start fixes should TIGHTEN this guard.

KNOWN GAP: warmed /deferred shell first-byte measured 1.57x react on 2026-07-20 (1.57ms vs 1.00ms). Both flavors beat the 40ms defer floor (genuinely streaming); the gap is pre-shell work. Tighten alongside warm_ttfb_posts.

KNOWN GAP: warmed sequential ms/request on / measured 2.37x react on 2026-07-20 (695 vs 1650 req/s) — per-request overhead in the octane Start SSR path. The 3.5 ceiling only catches further regression; fixes should TIGHTEN it.

Host-overhead guard, renderer-independent: the nitro deployment output vs the identical app behind the minimal host measured 1.13x on 2026-07-20. A breach is a nitro/deployment regression, not an octane one.
